claimText: Vikram-1 is a private orbital-class launch vehicle developed by the Indian space-startup Skyroot Aerospace.
evidenceExcerpt: Vikram-1 is India's new private orbital-class launch vehicle developed by Skyroot Aerospace.
examUse: Statement-based question identifying the developers of India's private launch vehicles.

claimText: Vikram-1 is designed to launch small satellites and rideshare missions into Low Earth Orbit (LEO) or Sun-Synchronous Orbit (SSO).
evidenceExcerpt: Designed to launch small satellites and rideshare missions into Low Earth Orbit (LEO) or Sun-Synchronous Orbit (SSO).
examUse: Statement-based question on the target orbits and payload types of Vikram-1.

claimText: Vikram-1 is a four-stage rocket comprising three solid-fuel booster stages and a final liquid-propellant stage powered by a Raman engine.
evidenceExcerpt: It is a four-stage rocket: the first three stages use solid-fuel boosters. The final (upper) stage uses a liquid-propellant Raman engine for precise orbital insertion.
examUse: Statement-based question on the propulsion technology and staging of Vikram-1.

claimText: The Vikram-1 rocket is constructed with an all-carbon-fibre body and incorporates 3D-printed engines.
evidenceExcerpt: Features an all-carbon-fibre body and 3D-printed engines for a lightweight, efficient design.
examUse: Statement-based question on the materials and manufacturing processes used in modern private rockets.
